origin.name: Croatia
origin.source: FCDO Croatia travel advice, updated 2025; British Embassy Zagreb guidance
origin.cremation_note: Cremation in Croatia is available. Bringing ashes home to the UK is often simpler than full body repatriation. You will need the Smrtovnica, cremation certificate, and urn transport documentation.

destinations.name: United Kingdom
key_documents: Certified English translation of death certificate
special_rules: Croatia is an EU member and Hague Apostille Convention member, which simplifies document legalisation.
Death must be registered at the local Matični ured promptly.
All Croatian-language documentation requires a certified English translation for UK acceptance.
The State Attorney's Office orders post-mortem for suspicious or violent deaths.
destinations.dest_reception: The UK funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. All Croatian documentation must be in certified English translation. The coroner for the district is notified. Straightforward cases proceed directly to funeral arrangements.
destinations.dest_consular_note: FCDO 24-hour emergency line: +44 (0)20 7008 5000. The British Embassy in Zagreb can register the death and advise on local funeral directors. They cannot pay for or arrange repatriation.
destinations.seo_title: Croatia to UK Repatriation: Guidance for Families
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in Croatia. Repatriation to the UK takes 1-2 weeks. Smrtovnica process, apostille, and air cargo all handled. Contact us 24/7.
